label: Product Sustainability Parameters as Percentage of Turnover

sections.about: This metric measures the share of turnover from products/services that carry information on environmental and social parameters relevant to the product. It is a BRSR Principle 9 Essential Indicator under consumer responsibility, linked to product transparency and responsible consumption. A higher value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: The benchmark shows a wide disclosure-coverage gap rather than a simple steelmaking-performance gap. JSW reported 100% across FY2023-FY2025, while SAIL reported 100% in FY2023 and FY2024 before falling to 0% in FY2025; Jindal Stainless moved from 0% in FY2023 to 100% in FY2024 and FY2025. Tata Steel remained partial at 50% in FY2023, 53% in FY2024, and 52% in FY2025. Jindal’s FY2025 position is supported by product-level disclosures, customer education, product brochures, technical visits, fabricator training, display vans, and labels carrying grade, batch, heat number, dimensions, certification logos, REACH/RoHS references and BIS/ISI marks for multiple stainless grades.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranks 3rd at 52%, behind JSW and Jindal at 100%; the gap to the best performer is 48 percentage points, or 48% below the 100% benchmark. Tata’s rank improved from 3rd in FY2023 to neither leading nor catching up in FY2024 despite a numeric rise to 53%, because Jindal moved to 100%; Tata then remained 3rd in FY2025 as SAIL fell to 0%. The underlying issue appears to be disclosure coverage and product-information mapping rather than absence of product responsibility systems, because Tata reports value-chain policy coverage, ResponsibleSteel, BIS, ISO and related certifications, and sustainable procurement practices, but these do not translate into 100% turnover coverage for this specific P9 product-information metric.
sections.targets: Related BRSR commitments include Tata Steel’s Net Zero by 2045 ambition, low-emission EAF transition, and scrap-use expansion, while Jindal Stainless discloses a 50% carbon-emission-intensity reduction target by 2035 versus FY2022 baseline, Net Zero by 2050, 100% supplier ESG assessment ambition, and ResponsibleSteel certification ambition.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Tata Steel FY2022-23 is consolidated, while later Tata figures include restatements and boundary changes linked to mergers. SAIL’s FY2025 fall from 100% to 0% should be read as a disclosure/classification movement unless corroborated by a detailed methodology note, not as proof of a sudden product-portfolio change.
